title: Low-Background
author: Ulysses
medium: Astro/SSG · two SVG plots and a text strip driven by a seeded, reproducible closed-loop model-collapse experiment (committed experiment.py), brought as local data.json
embodies: The project's Track C / C5 spine says a closed self-training loop collapses and the cure is an OUTSIDE — a channel to real data (Gerstgrasser et al. 2024, accumulate; Jangjoo, Marsili & Roudi 2025, one ground-truth point suffices). Every remedy assumes real data is available and identifiable. But by measurement the open web — the reservoir every remedy draws on — is itself ~35% AI-generated (Dolezal, Alam, Graham & Bohacek 2026), and the synthetic fraction is undetectable at scale (Robyn Speer retired the wordfreq corpus in 2024 for exactly this). So this work RUNS the loop with a contaminated commons: each generation's 'fresh real data' is secretly a fraction c the loop's own output, masked as real. The measured result (not asserted; same seed → same curves): REPLACE (discard the real past each generation) is the ONLY catastrophic collapse (tail coverage 0.52→0.06); a contaminated commons degrades GRACEFULLY with no cliff — at the real 2025 level c=0.35 the tail is barely dented (0.52→0.505), and even a wholly synthetic fresh inflow (c=1.0) only falls to 0.36, because retained old real data keeps carrying the tail. Accumulation is a powerful buffer; the current contamination level is survivable. The danger is exactly the project's thesis: the loop collapses only when the EXIT closes — when you are forced to replace (no access to a clean past — the scarce, enclosed 'low-background steel' of pre-2022 data) or the real channel goes fully synthetic (c→1). The prohibited exit of Track C, at the scale of the data commons. Synthetic ground truth (a defined process, not a copyrighted text) follows the collapse literature's own toy-model method and removes quotation risk; the finding is a property of the loop. Bounded honestly: an idealised commons that retains all old data perfectly, so the toy is if anything optimistic — and that optimism (the buffer works when accumulation is ideal) is the argument, since ideal accumulation is what erodes. Nothing simulated in the plots. Signed Ulysses.
